devanagari: अंबरीषानुग्रहेण दुर्वाससो दुःखनिवृत्तिः अंबरीशप्रशंसा तस्य भगवाद्रूपतापत्तीश्च । श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं भगवताऽदिष्टो दुर्वासाश्चक्रतापितः । अ...
english_devnagari: aṃbarīṣānugraheṇa durvāsaso duḥkhanivṛttiḥ aṃbarīśapraśaṃsā tasya bhagavādrūpatāpattīśca śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ bhagavatā aṃbarīṣaṃ upāvṛtya tatpādau duḥkhito

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Being thus commanded by the glorious Lord, Durvāsas who was afflicted by the discus Sudarśana, returned to Ambarīṣa, and sought protection at his feet (by touching them) remorsefully.

devanagari: तस्य सोद्यमनं वीक्ष्य पादस्पर्शविलज्जितः । अस्तावीत् तत् हरेः अस्त्रं कृपया पीडितो भृशम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya sodyamanaṃ vīkṣya pādasparśavilajjitaḥ astāvīt tat hareḥ astraṃ kṛpayā pīḍito bhṛśam
translation: Observing the act (of supplication and touching his feet) by Durvāsas, Ambarīṣa felt ashamed at the touch of his feet (by the sage), and being overwhelmed with deep compassion, prayed to the missile of Lord Hari.

devanagari: अंबरीष उवाच । त्वमग्निर्भगवान् सूर्यः त्वं सोमो ज्योतिषां पतिः । त्वं आपस्त्वं क्षितिर्व्योम वायुर्मात्रेन्द्रियाणि च ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: aṃbarīṣa uvāca tvamagnirbhagavān sūryaḥ tvaṃ somo jyotiṣāṃ patiḥ tvaṃ āpastvaṃ kṣitirvyoma vāyurmātrendriyāṇi ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

translation:
Ambarīṣa prayed:
You are the glorious Fire-god, the Sun-god, Soma (or Deity presiding over the moon), the Lord of luminaries (constellations of stars, planets). You are (the elements like) water, the earth, the sky, the air. You are the subtle elements and the senses (or the objects of the senses and fire etc. function due to your power).

devanagari: सुदर्शन नमस्तुभ्यं सहस्राराच्युतप्रिय । सर्वास्त्रघातिन् विप्राय स्वस्ति भूया इडस्पते ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sudarśana namastubhyaṃ sahasrārācyutapriya sarvāstraghātin viprāya svasti bhūyā iḍaspate
translation: Oh Sudarśana! I pay my respects to you who are of thousand spokesand so dear to The Imperishable Lord. You are the destroyer of all missiles, and protector of the earth. May you be propitious to the Brāhmaṇa.

devanagari: त्वं धर्मस्त्वमृतं सत्यं त्वं यज्ञोऽखिलयज्ञभुक् । त्वं लोकपालः सर्वात्मा त्वं तेजः पौरुषं परम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ dharmastvamṛtaṃ satyaṃ tvaṃ yajño tvaṃ lokapālaḥ sarvātmā tvaṃ tejaḥ pauruṣaṃ param
translation: You are the righteousness (dharma) itself, the Divine Law (ṛta) and the Truth (incarnate). You are the sacrifice, and you are the presiding Deity of the Sacrifice who enjoys the oblations. You are the protector of regions, the soul (the Inner Controller) of all; you are the splendour and energy of the Supreme Person.

devanagari: नमः सुनाभाखिलधर्मसेतवे । ह्यधर्मशीलासुरधूमकेतवे । त्रैलोक्यगोपाय विशुद्धवर्चसे । मनोजवायाद्‍भुतकर्मणे गृणे ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: namaḥ sunābhākhiladharmasetave hyadharmaśīlāsuradhūmaketave trailokyagopāya viśuddhavarcase manojavāyād‍bhutakarmaṇe gṛṇe
translation: Oh beautifully centred discus! (Nobody being able to glorify you adequately) I simply utter the word “Bow” to you who are the defender of the injunctions of the Divine Law (ominous and destructive like) a comet unto the unrighteous and the Asuras, the Protector of the three worlds, endowed with purest splendour, of quick speed like the flight of thought and of miraculous deeds.

devanagari: त्वत्तेजसा धर्ममयेन संहृतं । तमः प्रकाशश्च दृशो महात्मनाम् । दुरत्ययस्ते महिमा गिरां पते । त्वद् रूपमेतत् सदसत् परावरम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tvattejasā dharmamayena saṃhṛtaṃ tamaḥ prakāśaśca dṛśo mahātmanām duratyayaste mahimā girāṃ pate tvad rūpametat sadasat parāvaram
translation: Oh Lord of speech! By your splendour (constituted) of righteousness, darkness of ignorance obscuring the vision (or the knowledge of the Deity) has been dispelled, and the light (of god-realization) has been flashed and preserved, for the noble-souled righteous people. This entire universe, gross or subtle, high or low has been lighted by you (through the sun, the moon and other luminaries). Your greatness is simply unfathomable.

devanagari: यदा विसृष्टस्त्वमनञ्जनेन वै । बलं प्रविष्टोऽजित दैत्यदानवम् । बाहूदरोर्वङ्घ्रिशिरोधराणि । वृक्णन् अजस्रं प्रधने विराजसे ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā visṛṣṭastvamanañjanena vai balaṃ praviṣṭo bāhūdarorvaṅghriśirodharāṇi vṛkṇan ajasraṃ pradhane virājase

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Oh Invincible one![1] when discharged by the blemishless Lord, you enter the army of the Daityas and Dānavas and severing (incessantly) their arms, bowels, thighs and feet and necks, you shine irresistible in battles.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
v.l. ūrjita: the (army) of mighty Daityas and Dānavas.

devanagari: स त्वं जगत्त्राण खलप्रहाणये । निरूपितः सर्वसहो गदाभृता । विप्रस्य चास्मत् कुलदैवहेतवे । विधेहि भद्रं तदनुग्रहो हि नः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tvaṃ jagattrāṇa khalaprahāṇaye nirūpitaḥ sarvasaho gadābhṛtā viprasya cāsmat kuladaivahetave vidhehi bhadraṃ tadanugraho hi naḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: Oh protector of the world! You who overcome all (enemies in battle), have been commissioned by the wielder of the mace (Lord Viṣṇu) for the annihilation of the wicked. Therefore, for the sake of the prosperity of our family, be pleased to grant welfare (protection) to the Brāhmaṇa (Durvāsas). That will be your grace unto us (otherwise we shall be responsible for committing the sin of killing a Brāhmaṇa)

devanagari: यद्यस्ति दत्तमिष्टं वा स्वधर्मो वा स्वनुष्ठितः । कुलं नो विप्रदैवं चेद् द्विजो भवतु विज्वरः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: yadyasti dattamiṣṭaṃ vā svadharmo vā svanuṣṭhitaḥ kulaṃ no vipradaivaṃ ced dvijo bhavatu vijvaraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ yaj class 1 verb]

translation: If a charitable gift has been donated (by us), or a sacrifice has been performed, or our righteous duty has been properly executed, or if our family regards Brāhmaṇas as our gods, may this Brāhmaṇa be free from his distress.

devanagari: यदि नो भगवान् प्रीत एकः सर्वगुणाश्रयः । सर्वभूतात्मभावेन द्विजो भवतु विज्वरः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: yadi no bhagavān prīta ekaḥ sarvaguṇāśrayaḥ sarvabhūtātmabhāvena dvijo bhavatu vijvaraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation: If the glorious lord, the sole abode of all excellent qualities be pleased with us due to our looking upon (regarding) all beings as possessing souls like us (or his Spirit), may this Brāhmaṇa get rid of his distress and anxiety.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति संस्तुवतो राज्ञो विष्णुचक्रं सुदर्शनम् । अशाम्यत् सर्वतो विप्रं प्रदहद् राजयाच्ञया ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca iti saṃstuvato rājño viṣṇucakraṃ sudarśanam aśāmyat sarvato vipraṃ pradahad rājayācñayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
While the king was thus praying to Viṣṇu’s discus Sudarśana, which was scorching the Brāhmaṇa on all sides, it subsided at the request of the king.

devanagari: स मुक्तोऽस्त्राग्नितापेन दुर्वासाः स्वस्तिमान् ततः । प्रशशंस तमुर्वीशं युञ्जानः परमाशिषः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa mukto praśaśaṃsa tamurvīśaṃ yuñjānaḥ paramāśiṣaḥ
translation: Being relieved of the scorching heat of the fire of the missile (Sudarśana), Durvāsas felt comfortable. Bestowing on him the highest blessings, he praised the king.

devanagari: दुर्वासा उवाच । अहो अनन्तदासानां महत्त्वं दृष्टमद्य मे । कृतागसोऽपि यद् राजन् मंगलानि समीहसे ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: durvāsā uvāca aho anantadāsānāṃ mahattvaṃ dṛṣṭamadya me kṛtāgaso

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation:
Durvāsas said:
“Oh! How wonderful! I have witnessed today the magnanimity of servants of the Infinite Lord in that you wish for the well-being of a person who has offended you, Oh king.

devanagari: दुष्करः को नु साधूनां दुस्त्यजो वा महात्मनाम् । यैः संगृहीतो भगवान् सात्वतां ऋषभो हरिः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: duṣkaraḥ ko nu sādhūnāṃ dustyajo vā mahātmanām yaiḥ saṃgṛhīto bhagavān sātvatāṃ ṛṣabho hariḥ
translation: What is difficult to be achieved for those righteous persons or what is impossible to give up in the case of those noble-souled persons, who have treasured up (and made their own) the glorious Lord Hari, the leader of the Sātvatas.

devanagari: यन्नामश्रुतिमात्रेण पुमान् भवति निर्मलः । तस्य तीर्थपदः किं वा दासानां अवशिष्यते ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: yannāmaśrutimātreṇa pumān bhavati nirmalaḥ tasya tīrthapadaḥ kiṃ vā dāsānāṃ avaśiṣyate
translation: Gan there be any deficiency or unfulfilment in the case of the servants of the Lord of the hallowing feet (to which even holy rivers resort for purification) by listening to the name of whom a man is absolved of all sins and impurities.,

devanagari: राजन् अनुगृहीतोऽहं त्वयातिकरुणात्मना । मद् अघं पृष्ठतः कृत्वा प्राणा यन्मेऽभिरक्षिताः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: rājan anugṛhīto mad aghaṃ pṛṣṭhataḥ kṛtvā prāṇā yanme

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Oh King! I am highly favoured by you in that, exceedingly merciful as you are, you ignored the wrong done by me, and have completely saved my life.”

devanagari: राजा तं अकृताहारः प्रत्यागमनकांक्षया । चरणौ उवुपसंगृह्य प्रसाद्य समभोजयत् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: rājā taṃ akṛtāhāraḥ pratyāgamanakāṃkṣayā caraṇau uvupasaṃgṛhya prasādya samabhojayat

[nominative single from √ bhuj ], [vocative single from √ bhuj ], [accusative single from √ bhuj ]

translation: The king who,did not take any food but waited in expectation of the sage’s return, clasped his feet, conciliated him, and made him accept the meal.

devanagari: सोऽशित्वादृतमानीतं आतिथ्यं सार्वकामिकम् । तृप्तात्मा नृपतिं प्राह भुज्यतां इति सादरम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: so tṛptātmā nṛpatiṃ prāha bhujyatāṃ iti sādaram
translation: Having enjoyed the hospitality and the food that was suitable to all tastes and (thus) gratifying all desires) and was served with respect and care, he felt himself thoroughly gratified, and courteously requested the king to take his meals.

devanagari: प्रीतोऽस्मि अनुगृहीतोऽस्मि तव भागवतस्य वै । दर्शनस्पर्शनालापैः आतिथ्येनात्ममेधसा ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: prīto darśanasparśanālāpaiḥ ātithyenātmamedhasā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: “I am thoroughly pleased with you, and have been laid under great obligations by the sight, touch, conversation as well as by the hospitality-offered with full devotion to the Lord—of a great votary of the Lord (like you).

devanagari: कर्मावदातं एतत् ते गायन्ति स्वःस्त्रियो मुहुः । कीर्तिं परमपुण्यां च कीर्तयिष्यति भूरियम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: karmāvadātaṃ etat te gāyanti svaḥstriyo muhuḥ kīrtiṃ paramapuṇyāṃ ca kīrtayiṣyati bhūriyam
translation: The celestial damsels will often sing of this noble behaviour (act) of yours (in heaven), and this earth (the whole world) will extol this highly hallowing glory of yours.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं संकीर्त्य राजानं दुर्वासाः परितोषितः । ययौ विहायसाऽमंत्र्य ब्रह्मलोकमहैतुकम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ saṃkīrtya rājānaṃ durvāsāḥ paritoṣitaḥ yayau vihāyasā

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having thus praised the king, Durvāsas who was highly satisfied, took his leave of him and through ethereal space went to the region of Brahmā[2] which lasts for two Parārdha years.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
ahaitukam [ahaituka]—where dry rationalists have no place—Anvitārthaprakāśikā.

Footnote [2]:
ahaitukam [ahaituka]—where dry rationalists have no place—Anvitārthaprakāśikā.

devanagari: संवत्सरोऽत्यगात् तावद् यावता नागतो गतः । मुनिस्तद्दर्शनाकांक्षो राजाऽब्भक्षो बभूव ह ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃvatsaro munistaddarśanākāṃkṣo rājā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: A complete year elapsed since the departed sage Durvāsas did not return. It is reported that during this period, the king who longed to see him returned, subsisted simply on water.

devanagari: गतेऽथ दुर्वाससि सोऽम्बरीषो । द्विजोपयोगातिपवित्रमाहरत् । ऋषेर्विमोक्षं व्यसनं च बुद्ध्वा । मेने स्ववीर्यं च परानुभावम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: gate dvijopayogātipavitramāharat ṛṣervimokṣaṃ vyasanaṃ ca buddhvā mene svavīryaṃ ca parānubhāvam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: After the departure of Durvāsas, Ambarīṣa ate the (remnant of the) food that was rendered extremely holy by being partaken of by the Brāhmaṇa (Durvāsas). Pondering over the calamity on Durvāsas and his final release and his own fortitude, he considered all that being due to the power of the Supreme Person.

devanagari: एवं विधानेकगुणः स राजा । परात्मनि ब्रह्मणि वासुदेवे । क्रियाकलापैः समुवाह भक्तिं । ययाऽविरिञ्च्यान् निरयांश्चकार ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vidhānekaguṇaḥ sa rājā parātmani brahmaṇi vāsudeve kriyākalāpaiḥ samuvāha bhaktiṃ yayā

translation: The king Ambarīṣa who was thus endowed with such ennumerable [innumerable?] excellent qualities, continued in his devotion of the Lord by dedicating all his acts suitable to his own caste and stage of life to the Supreme Soul, Brahman designated as Vāsudeva. By virtue of such (firm) devotion, he came to regard all positions upto the part of god Brahmā as hellish.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । अथाम्बरीषस्तनयेषु राज्यं । समानशीलेषु विसृज्य धीरः । वनं विवेशात्मनि वासुदेवे । मनो दधद् ध्वस्तगुणप्रवाहः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca athāmbarīṣastanayeṣu rājyaṃ samānaśīleṣu visṛjya dhīraḥ vanaṃ viveśātmani vāsudeve mano dadhad dhvastaguṇapravāhaḥ

translation: In course of time, Ambarīṣa entrusted his kingdom to his sons whose disposition and character were similar to him, and entered the forest. Concentrating his mind wholly on Vāsudeva, his own soul, he destroyed (became free from) the current of guṇas viz. Saṃsāra or transmigration of the soul.

devanagari: इत्येतत् पुण्यमाख्यानं अंबरीषस्य भूपतेः । संकीर्तयन् अनुध्यायन् भक्तो भगवतो भवेत् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: ityetat puṇyamākhyānaṃ aṃbarīṣasya bhūpateḥ saṃkīrtayan anudhyāyan bhakto bhagavato bhavet

translation:
He who glorifies this sacred story of king Ambarīṣa or ponders over it, becomes a devotee of Lord Viṣṇu.
27(A). Those who listen to the story of the noble-souled Ambarīṣa, all of them attain to Liberation from Sarñsāra through the devotion to and by the Grace of Lord Viṣṇu.
